draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from a cozy, approachable left navigation—creating intimate touchpoints rather than corporate sterility
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward inviting, human-scaled interfaces; favor cream/tan/rust tones; softer typography; personality in navigation
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, neutral, or cool-toned hierarchy; minimize visual warmth; prioritize clarity over coziness; crisp grays/blues

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from a cozy, approachable left navigation—creating intimate touchpoints rather than corporate sterility
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward inviting, human-scaled interfaces; favor cream/tan/rust tones; softer typography; personality in navigation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer human-centered, approachable interfaces over corporate coldness
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warm neutrals, cream, soft gold, wood tones, and inviting typography for CRM dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Test cool, minimal, or high-contrast interfaces; consider if freelancers want business-formal over cozy-professional

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need quick client overview without deep drilling; a persistent sidebar navigation paired with a scrollable card-based client list reduces cognitive load vs tabbed or grid layouts
facade.label: Sidebar + card list
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and scannable client summaries; likely prefers hierarchical info architecture and modal-based actions over in-line editing
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer self-contained layouts (tabs, grids) or denser information presentation; navigation should be less persistent or card format is too sparse for their workflow

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers starting a CRM need to decide their primary view—do they scan many clients at once (dashboard with key metrics) or work through clients one at a time (detailed list view)?
facade.label: Dashboard or List
facade.acceptImplies: User wants high-level snapshot of all client relationships at a glance; values overview-first design with cards, summaries, and key metrics visible without scrolling.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants linear, sequential interaction; prefers diving deep into one client record at a time; favors detailed forms and chronological views over aggregated data.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/metrics focus—reveals if they prioritize relationship management or business analytics
Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/metrics focus—reveals if they prioritize relationship management or business analytics
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they expect instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and delight
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a warm, conversational tone that feels like talking to a friend rather than enterprise software
facade.label: Casual and chatty
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use informal language, contractions, emoji, and personality in UI copy and error messages. Build for approachability over corporate polish.
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should adopt a more formal, structured tone—clean language, professional terminology, minimal personality. Users value clarity and expertise over warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer immediate visual confirmation over smooth animations when updating client records
facade.label: Instant feedback
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should prioritize snappy, instant state changes (checkmarks pop, data saves with no loader, clicks register immediately) over polished transitions
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should lean into fluid animations and staged transitions (smooth loaders, easing curves, choreographed state changes) to feel premium and build confidence in async operations

facade.hypothesis: A freelancer CRM thrives when information density is low and scannable—overwhelming dashboards drive churn, while calm clarity builds daily habit
facade.label: Minimal dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize white space, progressive disclosure, and one-thing-at-a-time workflows; avoid chart clusters and dense widgets
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect rich data visualization and comprehensive overview; design for information density and real-time monitoring feeds

draft.title: Freelance CRM Dashboard
draft.summary: A clean, mobile-first CRM interface for freelancers to track clients, projects, and invoices. Warm, professional aesthetic with intuitive navigation.
